evidence.claim: [MTOR] mTOR kinase (the direct target) — VERDICT: UNAMBIGUOUSLY RESCUE - canonical on-target (severe constraint is the main concern). MTOR is the canonical on-target node; knockdown/inhibition unambiguously normalizes the TSC2-KO/mTORC1 phenotype (STRING direct interactions with RPTOR/RICTOR/RHEB/RPS6KB1/EIF4EBP1 ~0.999); severe gene constraint (pLI~1.0) is the primary tolerability concern.

[CDK9] Cyclin-dependent kinase 9 / P-TEFb / transcription elongation — VERDICT: MOST PLAUSIBLE RESCUE CANDIDATE - direct mTORC1 pathway intersection, with caveats. CDK9/P-TEFb directly intersects the mTORC1 pathway (transcription elongation control); the blind pass ranks CDK9 the most plausible knockdown-rescue candidate, with on-target toxicity caveats.

[FZD7] Frizzled class receptor 7 / WNT receptor — VERDICT: PLAUSIBLE PARTIAL RESCUE (context-dependent; potentially worsening via WNT). FZD7 activates Wnt->Dvl->beta-catenin and Wnt->PI3K->mTOR; Wnt/mTOR crosstalk is bidirectional, so FZD7 knockdown could partially dampen mTOR drive in some contexts but may also worsen.

[BCL2] Anti-apoptotic regulator / autophagy gate — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE to WORSENING. BCL2 is a downstream anti-apoptotic effector; mTORC1 suppresses autophagy and BCL2-BECN1-mTOR autophagy crosstalk is extensive, so BCL2 knockdown does not address upstream mTORC1 and may worsen.

[VPS54] GARP complex / retrograde Golgi trafficking — VERDICT: VERY LIKELY INEFFECTIVE - no mechanistic link to mTORC1. VPS54 tethers retrograde endosome-to-TGN vesicle traffic as part of the GARP complex; essentially no published mechanistic link to mTORC1.

[KMT2D] H3K4 methyltransferase / MLL4 complex — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE TO POTENTIALLY WORSENING - extreme constraint, indirect link. KMT2D and mTOR co-mutate in cancer; KMT2D is extremely constrained, and its loss can derepress mTOR (knockdown may worsen rather than rescue).

[DPM2] Dolichol-phosphate mannose synthase regulatory subunit — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE - no mTORC1 mechanistic connection. DPM2 is the most gnomAD-tolerant gene in the panel but has no TSC2/mTORC1 mechanistic basis; any ER-stress-mediated effect is speculative.

[RPS3] 40S ribosomal protein S3 — VERDICT: STRONGLY WORSENING - essential ribosome component. RPS3 is an essential ribosomal protein and a downstream effector of mTORC1-driven translation; knockdown would strongly worsen/collapse translation, not rescue. Also has extra-ribosomal DNA-damage/NF-kB roles.

[SSU72] RNA Pol II CTD phosphatase — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE - unrelated to mTOR; insertional oncogenesis risk. SSU72 is an RNA Pol II CTD phosphatase facilitating reinitiation/termination; no TSC2/mTOR link in STRING/Reactome/literature.

[DIDO1] Death inducer-obliterator 1 / chromatin factor — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE - chromatin regulator; no mTOR basis. DIDO1 is a highly constrained H3K4me3 reader / splicing factor with obesity-linked biology and no TSC2/mTOR mechanistic connection.

[ACTR3] Arp3 / Arp2/3 branched-actin nucleation — VERDICT: WORSENING - essential cytoskeletal gene. ACTR3 (Arp3) is a pan-essential cytoskeletal nucleator; knockdown impairs cytokinesis/endocytosis globally with no mTOR rescue mechanism (knockdown lethal).

[NCOA6] Nuclear receptor coactivator 6 / PRIP — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE for mTOR rescue (may modulate NLRP3 inflammasome). NCOA6 has at most an indirect PI3K/AKT/mTOR transcriptional link and is highly constrained; judged ineffective for mTOR rescue (separate NLRP3-inflammasome effect possible).

[SMARCE1] SWI/SNF (BAF) chromatin remodeler subunit — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE; potential independent oncogenic risk. In clear-cell meningioma SMARCE1 LOSS activates mTOR, so SMARCE1 knockdown would tend to worsen mTOR; highly constrained tumor-suppressor context.

[SAP18] Sin3A-associated protein 18 / ASAP splicing complex — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE - no mTOR basis; high brain expression = toxicity concern. SAP18 is a splicing/transcription factor with no mTOR mechanistic basis and very high CNS expression, so knockdown raises neurotoxicity/confounding concerns rather than rescue.

[PSMD13] 26S proteasome regulatory subunit (Rpn9) — VERDICT: WORSENING - pan-essential proteasome component. PSMD13 (Rpn9) is a pan-essential 26S proteasome subunit; mTORC1 hyperactivation already stresses proteostasis, so PSMD13 knockdown worsens proteostatic collapse rather than rescuing.
